text.title: Claude Advanced Features Decision Tree
text.summary: Decision tree gating which advanced Claude feature(s) apply to the current workload. Root question is a latency observable; branches name concrete observables (`when`) and conclude with a rule id from 01-core-rules.xml or a skip directive.

decision-tree.root-question: Does the workload tolerate ≥minutes latency AND is it non-user-facing (offline enrichment / nightly analysis)?

branch.conclusion: Use Batch API per rule r5.

branch.when: no AND system_prompt_tokens >= 1024 AND prefix_stable=true AND calls_per_session >= 10
branch.conclusion: Wire Prompt Caching per rule r3 and monitor per rule r4.
Enable Extended Thinking per rule r1; observe constraints from rule r2.
Enable Computer Use per rule r7.
Skip Computer Use — provision a sandbox first; revisit.
Skip Prompt Caching — write surcharge will exceed savings.
Skip Batch API and Extended Thinking — use the base Messages API path.

example.trace: root → no AND system_prompt_tokens >= 1024 AND prefix_stable=true AND calls_per_session >= 10 → r3
example.description: A long-document QA assistant calling Claude 50 times per session with a 6k-token doc as the system prefix matches the second branch; the tree concludes `r3`, so the agent must mark the doc with `cache_control:{"type":"ephemeral"}` and monitor the hit ratio per `r4`.
